title: Understanding Heat: From Kinetic Energy to Temperature
summary: Explore how heat is a form of energy resulting from the movement of particles and relate this understanding to temperature as well as everyday experiences.

sections.heading: What is Heat?
sections.body: Heat represents kinetic energy, or the motion, within a substance. When particles move faster and more vigorously—such as in hotter objects—they transfer this energy to other things they contact.
key_points: Heat is related to particle movement
It transfers from warmer to cooler bodies

sections.heading: The Nature of Temperature
sections.body: Temperature measures the average kinetic energy among particles in an object. A higher temperature indicates more intense motion, while a lower one suggests less activity at the molecular level.
key_points: Temperature is about averaged particle speed
It’s not just heat but how it's measured

sections.heading: Heat Transfer Methods
sections.body: Energy can transfer from one object to another through conduction, convection, or radiation. This concept explains natural phenomena and engineered systems alike.
key_points: There are three main methods of heat transfer
Each has a unique process for energy movement

key_facts.formula: Stefan-Boltzmann law: P = σAT^4, where 'P' is the power radiated from a black body
key_facts.fact: 'E=mc^2' also implies mass can be converted to energy
